name: Loop 8: Lake Mondely – 10 Pyrenean routes
name_original: Boucle 8 : Le lac de Mondely — 10 itinéraires pyrénéens
description:
This route is the most accessible of these 10 loops that I offer in Ariège. It starts in the village of Lescure to immediately cross the Baup and take the Piedmont V81 cycle route which connects Saint-Girons to Foix. This old railway line converted into a cycle path can be taken by road bike.

These are 17 kilometers of pure tranquillity. This bike path is made up of very fine gravel interspersed with tunnel crossings.

You leave this greenway by a tiny little busy secondary road to reach the Mondely reservoir. A place conducive to relaxation. After having taken advantage of this preserved space, you find the road which takes you to the village of Mas d'Azil, where another tunnel, this time natural, awaits you. It is a cave dug by the Arize river and not railway tunnels. Do not forget your lamp to cross this one kilometer long passage.

On the other side, the cave house offers drinks, pies and dishes. It is very practical in case of a little hunger. The beautiful meadow below invites you to take a break here.

To end this route, you take a very beautiful road that runs along the Arize then the Pujol to Lescure. This is the guarantee of freshness all the way.
region_intro: Road cycling around Lescure is characterized by its mountainous terrain, offering a mix of challenging ascents and scenic descents through valleys. The region features high ground that provides extensive views, with routes often following undulating roads. Cyclists can expect varied landscapes, from open countryside to stretches alongside rivers, providing diverse riding experiences. The topography includes both sustained climbs and flatter sections, catering to different preferences. Best road cycling routes around Lescure The most popular road cycling route is Loop 8: Lake Mondely – 10 Pyrenean routes, a 32.3 miles (52.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 55 minutes to complete. This moderate route features significant elevation gain through the Pyrenean landscape. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Col de Saraillé – Biert Village loop from Eycheil, a difficult 36.0 miles (57.9 km) path. This route includes substantial climbs and descents, offering panoramic views of the surrounding mountains. Local road cyclists also love the Col de Port – Fountain at Col de Port loop from Eycheil, a 46.6 miles (75.0 km) trail leading through high mountain passes, often completed in about 3 hours 52 minutes. Road cycling around Lescure is defined by mountainous ascents, scenic valleys, and extensive high-ground views. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging climbs. The routes in Lescure are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 70 reviews. More than 3000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Lescure's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Lescure?
A: There are over 125 road cycling routes around Lescure, catering to various skill levels. This includes approximately 15 easy routes, 56 moderate routes, and 54 difficult routes, offering a wide range of experiences from scenic valley rides to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Lescure?
A: Road cycling around Lescure is characterized by its mountainous terrain, featuring a mix of challenging ascents and scenic descents through valleys. You'll find high ground offering extensive views, undulating roads, and varied landscapes from open countryside to stretches alongside rivers. The topography includes both sustained climbs and flatter sections, particularly in the Pyrenean foothills.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes in Lescure?
A: While many routes feature significant elevation, there are approximately 15 easy road cycling routes around Lescure that could be suitable for families or those seeking a less strenuous ride. These routes often follow flatter sections or gentler inclines, providing a pleasant experience for all ages.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can see while cycling around Lescure?
A: The region offers panoramic views from its high ground and mountain passes. For specific points of interest, consider routes that pass by natural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2204863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grotte du Mas-d'Azil&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6217576" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Couserans region&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter historical sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5278887" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Souribet Terraces&lt;/a&gt; or cycle paths like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2092636" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The tunnels of Véloroute 81&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Lescure?
A: Yes, many of the routes around Lescure are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1206198296/loop-8-lake-mondely-10-pyrenean-routes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop 8: Lake Mondely – 10 Pyrenean routes&lt;/a&gt;, or the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40133404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col de Port – Fountain at Col de Port loop from Eycheil&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through high mountain passes.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Lescure?
A: Given the mountainous terrain, the best time for road cycling in Lescure is typically from late spring through early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is generally more stable, and mountain passes are clear of snow, offering ideal conditions for both challenging climbs and scenic rides.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Lescure?
A: The road cycling routes in Lescure are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 70 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the region's mountainous ascents, scenic valleys, and the extensive high-ground views, which provide diverse and rewarding riding experiences.

Q: Are there any challenging routes for experienced road cyclists?
A: Absolutely. Lescure offers numerous challenging routes for experienced cyclists, with 54 routes rated as difficult. These often feature substantial climbs and descents, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10531377" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col de Saraillé – Biert Village loop from Eycheil&lt;/a&gt;, which includes significant elevation gain and panoramic mountain views.

Q: Can I find routes that are longer in duration for extended rides?
A: Yes, there are several longer routes available for those seeking extended rides. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41677162" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Core Pass – Col de Saraillé loop from Eycheil&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route spanning over 86 kilometers, typically taking around 4 hours and 45 minutes to complete, offering a substantial challenge.

Q: Are there any routes that follow rivers or offer riverside views?
A: Yes, some road cycling routes in the broader region of Lescure offer pleasant stretches alongside rivers. While specific routes are not detailed, the general topography includes diverse landscapes, and you can find sections that follow river courses, providing a different scenic experience compared to mountain ascents.

Q: What is the typical elevation gain on routes around Lescure?
A: The elevation gain varies significantly depending on the route's difficulty. Moderate routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1206198296/loop-8-lake-mondely-10-pyrenean-routes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop 8: Lake Mondely – 10 Pyrenean routes&lt;/a&gt; can have around 700 meters of elevation gain, while difficult routes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40133404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col de Port – Fountain at Col de Port loop from Eycheil&lt;/a&gt; can feature over 1300 meters of climbing.
tips:
This magnificent 44-kilometre greenway has been developed between Saint-Girons and Foix, replacing an old railway line. It has many tunnels which have the particularity of being equipped with motion detectors.

You will therefore be enlightened as you progress underground.
This dam lake is the ideal place to take a refreshment break, soak your legs, swim or even bivouac.

In this magnificent prehistoric cave, crossed in its entirety by both the Arize river and a road, prehistoric portable art dating from the Azilian was discovered. The most famous of his sculptures is undoubtedly the spear-thrower called the bird fawn.

In the middle of the cave, a museum opens its doors to visitors. Come and learn a little more about this period of the Upper Palaeolithic.

Be careful when crossing the cavity. A good lamp is mandatory, because some parts are very dark and the pavement on the side can be treacherous.
